text.title: Reference Program — Output Schema
text.summary: Pins the structure of `references.yaml` and the per-asset record. Sales ops and the reference router both consume this file.

forbidden.pattern: asset.permitted_uses contains paid_ads or PR_release without consent_date within the last 12 months
reference deployed cross-archetype (industry or headcount mismatch) without explicit override note
reference reused after client churn without renewed consent (client status != active AND last asset use after churn date)
asset.type == "logo" appears on the website with consent_date older than 12 months

allowed-transformations.transformation: Renew consent extends `expires_on` by 12 months; record the renewal event in the audit log.
Aggregate multiple peer calls under one reference_id; each call counts toward fatigue.
Demote a reference from "active" to "rest" when fatigue reaches the cap; auto-restore at the next quarter.

list.item: Discovery: do not deploy a named reference; share aggregated outcome stats only.
Proposal: 1-2 case studies matched to archetype; no peer call yet.
Late-stage / champion building: 1 peer call, matched by archetype, prepped with the brief in `templates/peer-call-brief.md`.
Procurement: 1 quote in proposal, never a logo on slide 1 without consent for procurement context.
Post-sale onboarding: invite the new client to peer-call cohort; this is how the next reference is grown.
Every active reference has consent_date within 12 months
Every active reference is tagged with all four archetype keys
Every asset has a permitted_uses list — no implicit assumptions
Expired assets are flagged and removed from website / decks
